title: Rose petal potpourri
subtitle: Dried rose petals, lavender, orange peel, and orris root with rose essential oil
excerpt: Orris root powder is the fixative that makes potpourri fragrance last for months rather than days. It absorbs the essential oil and releases it slowly, acting as a natural reservoir. Without it, the scent evaporates within a week. This blend uses the classic combination of rose and lavender with citrus as a brightness note.
sourceNotes: Potpourri formulation from traditional fragrance and herbal crafting references. Orris root fixative properties from perfumery literature.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow to cure for 1 to 2 weeks in a sealed container before displaying, for the best scent integration.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months of strong fragrance. Refresh every 4 to 6 weeks with 5 drops of rose essential oil stirred into the mixture. The potpourri itself keeps indefinitely as a decorative object; only the fragrance diminishes.

glossaryTerms.definition: Dried, powdered rhizome of the Florentine iris (Iris germanica var. florentina). Used as a fixative in potpourri and perfumery; it absorbs essential oils into its starch matrix and releases them slowly over months.

An ingredient in a fragrance blend that slows the evaporation of volatile aromatic compounds, extending the scent's longevity. Orris root is the most traditional plant-based fixative in potpourri making.

The 1 to 2 week resting period after mixing potpourri in a sealed container. During curing, the essential oil is fully absorbed by the orris root and the botanicals, and the separate scents integrate into a cohesive blend.

content.text: The key to long-lasting potpourri is the

at 10% of the blend absorbs the rose essential oil and releases it slowly over many months. Without it, the scent evaporates within days. After mixing, seal the potpourri in a jar or bag and allow it to
for 1 to 2 weeks before displaying. The scent will be noticeably stronger and more integrated after curing. Shelf life is 12 months of strong fragrance.

items.prepNote: Approximately 0.5 g; added to the orris root first

content.text: Place the orris root powder in the mixing bowl and add the rose essential oil drops. Stir well so the oil is absorbed into the powder. Add the dried rose petals, lavender, and orange peel and stir gently to combine. Transfer to a sealed jar and leave for 1 to 2 weeks to cure.
After curing, display in an open bowl or jar. Stir occasionally to release fresh fragrance. Refresh every 4 to 6 weeks with 5 drops of rose essential oil added directly to the bowl.

Display in a cool, dry room away from direct sunlight, which fades the colours. Shelf life is 12 months of strong fragrance. After that the potpourri can be refreshed with additional essential oil or composted. Keep away from pets, particularly cats, who may be sensitive to some essential oils.
